info.name: Penneo Collect API
info.description:
A collection for the Penneo Collect API — creating and managing forms, and retrieving submission data.

**Setup**
Add the following variables before running:
- `clientId` — your OAuth client ID
- `clientSecret` — your OAuth client secret
- `apiKey` — your Penneo API key
- `apiSecret` — your Penneo API secret

All other variables are set automatically as you run the requests in order.

**Flows**
1. Run **1. Get Access Token** first — valid for 10 minutes, re-run if you get a 401.
2. Run **Form Management** steps in order to create, publish, inspect, update, and archive a form.
3. Use **Data Retrieval** steps to fetch submission data and documents once a form has been submitted and signed.

`externalId` is saved automatically when you create a form. `casefileId` and `documentId` must be set manually — they come from a completed signing flow in the Penneo API collection.

variable.description: Base URL for the Penneo Collect API. Change to https://app.penneo.com/collect/api for production.
Base URL for the Penneo Sign API (used for fetching signed documents). Change to https://app.penneo.com for production.
Base URL for the Penneo OAuth server. Change to https://login.penneo.com for production.
Auto-set by request 1 pre-request script. Used internally for WSSE auth.
Auto-set by request 1 pre-request script. Used internally for WSSE auth.
Auto-set by request 1 pre-request script. Used internally for WSSE auth.
Auto-set by Create Form. Can also be set manually to target an existing form.
Auto-set by Create Prefilled Form Request. The public URL to share with the end-user.
Auto-set by List Submissions from the first result. Can also be set manually.
Auto-set by List Submissions from the first result. Can also be set manually.
Set manually — the document ID from a completed casefile. Available from the Penneo API collection (step 4).

exec: throw new Error('Missing required collection variables: ' + missing.join(', ') + '. Set them in the collection Variables tab.');
console.log('Access token saved. Expires in ' + json.expires_in + ' seconds.');

request.description:
Authenticates using the API Keys Grant flow. The pre-request script automatically calculates the nonce, timestamp, and SHA1 digest.

The access token is valid for 10 minutes and is saved to `accessToken` for all subsequent requests.

item.name: Collect Management
item.description:
Requests for creating and managing forms.

**Note:** If you update a form that is already published (ACTIVE), a new DRAFT version is created under the same `externalId`. You need to run **Publish Form** again to make the changes live.

request.description:
Creates a new form in DRAFT status. The `externalId` is automatically saved for use in all subsequent steps.

The form is not publicly accessible until it is published.

exec: if (!externalId) { throw new Error('externalId is not set. Run Create Form first, or set it manually in the Variables tab.'); }
console.log('Form ' + pm.collectionVariables.get('externalId') + ' published. It is now publicly accessible.');

request.description: Publishes the form, changing its status from DRAFT to ACTIVE. End-users can now access and submit it.

exec: if (!externalId) { throw new Error('externalId is not set. Run Create Form first, or set it manually in the Variables tab.'); }

request.description: Retrieves the full form definition including all sections, fields, status, and published URL (if active).

exec: if (!externalId) { throw new Error('externalId is not set. Run Create Form first, or set it manually in the Variables tab.'); }
console.log('Form updated. If the form was ACTIVE, a new DRAFT version has been created. Run Publish Form again to make it live.');

request.description:
Updates the form. If the form is currently ACTIVE, a new DRAFT version is created — run Publish Form again to make it live.

This example adds a Company Name field to the original form.

exec: if (!externalId) { throw new Error('externalId is not set. Run Create Form first, or set it manually in the Variables tab.'); }
console.log('Share this URL with the end-user to open the form with pre-populated values.');

request.description:
Creates a prefilled form request — the form opens with the specified field values already populated. The end-user can still edit any field where `editable` is true.

The answer keys (`full_name`, `email_address`) match the `name` properties set on the fields when the form was created. Update them if you use different field names.

The `requestUrl` is automatically saved. Share it with the end-user to let them access the pre-populated form.

item.name: Form Examples
item.description: Ready-to-run form examples demonstrating specific Collect API features. Each request creates a form in DRAFT status — run Publish Form from the Form Management folder to take it live.

request.description:
Creates a form demonstrating two types of conditional visibility:

**Conditional section** — The "Business Details" section (company name, registration number) is hidden until the user selects "Business" for Customer Type.

**Conditional field** — The "Agreement ID" field is hidden until the user selects "Yes" for the existing agreement question. It becomes required once visible.

Both use a single `EQUALS` rule. The `field` value in each rule references the `name` of an earlier field.

Creates a form with a `caseFileTitleTemplate` that controls how the resulting casefile is named in Penneo Sign.

The template supports merge fields that are resolved at submission time:
- `{{formName}}` — the name of the form
- `{{primarySigner.name}}` — the signer's full name
- `{{primarySigner.email}}` — the signer's email address

**Note:** These are Penneo merge fields, not Postman variables. Postman will leave them as-is in the request body since they are not defined as collection variables.

Creates a form demonstrating merge fields in HTML content. The third section contains an HTML field that references answers from earlier fields by their `name` property using `{{fieldName}}` syntax.

The "Application Summary" section dynamically renders a review block showing the applicant's name, email, loan amount, purpose, and repayment period — all pulled from earlier answers.

**How merge field keys work:** Use `{{name}}` where `name` is the `name` property of the source field. The source field must be required and non-conditional.

Creates a form demonstrating the `mapTo` property. Two fields are mapped to primary signer properties:

- `"mapTo": "primarySigner.name"` on the Full Name field
- `"mapTo": "primarySigner.email"` on the Email Address field

When the form filler submits, Penneo uses these values to automatically identify them as the primary signer — no separate signer setup or `predefinedSigners` configuration required.

**Constraint:** The field mapped to any `*.email` property must be of type `EMAIL`.

exec: if (!externalId) { throw new Error('externalId is not set. Run Create Form first, or set it manually in the Variables tab.'); }
console.log('Form ' + pm.collectionVariables.get('externalId') + ' archived. It is no longer publicly accessible. Existing submissions are preserved.');

request.description:
Archives the form, changing its status from ACTIVE to ARCHIVED. The public URL becomes inactive and no new submissions are accepted.

Existing submissions are preserved and can still be retrieved via the Data Retrieval steps.

exec: if (!externalId) { throw new Error('externalId is not set. Run Create Form first, or set it manually in the Variables tab.'); }
console.log('Multiple submissions (' + json.items.length + '). Variables set to the first.');

query.description: Maximum number of results to return (1–1000).
Number of results to skip for pagination.

request.description:
Returns all submissions for the given form. Only completed, processed submissions are included, ordered by submission ID ascending.

Automatically saves `submissionId` and `casefileId` from the first result. If there are multiple submissions, update those variables manually to work with a specific one.

Use `limit` and `offset` to paginate through large result sets.

exec: if (!externalId) { throw new Error('externalId is not set. Run Create Form first, or set it manually in the Variables tab.'); }
if (!submissionId) { throw new Error('submissionId is not set. Run List Submissions first, or set it manually in the Variables tab.'); }

request.description: Retrieves all form field answers submitted by the end-user for a specific submission.

exec: if (!casefileId) { throw new Error('casefileId is not set. Run List Submissions first, or set it manually in the Variables tab.'); }

request.description:
Lists all files (e.g. uploaded attachments from FILE fields) attached to a specific casefile, including filename, size, and download link.

**Requires `casefileId`** — set it manually in the collection Variables tab.

exec: if (!casefileId) { throw new Error('casefileId is not set. Run List Submissions first, or set it manually in the Variables tab.'); }
console.log('Multiple documents found (' + ids.length + '). documentId set to first: ' + ids[0] + '. Update manually for others.');
console.warn('No documents found in casefile — signing may not be complete yet.');

request.description:
Retrieves the full casefile details from the Penneo Sign API, including status, signers, and documents.

Automatically saves `documentId` from the first document in the response — used by Get Signed Document.

Uses `signBaseUrl` (https://sandbox.penneo.com) rather than the Collect API base URL.

exec: if (!documentId) { throw new Error('documentId is not set. Run Get Casefile Details first, or set it manually in the Variables tab.'); }
console.log('Signed document downloaded. Size: ' + pm.response.responseSize + ' bytes.');

request.description:
Downloads the signed PDF document. Set the response type to 'Send and Download' in Postman to save the file locally.

This uses `signBaseUrl` (https://sandbox.penneo.com) rather than the Collect API base URL, as it calls the Penneo Sign API — the same endpoint as in the Penneo API collection.

**Requires `documentId`** — set it manually in the collection Variables tab. The document ID is available from step 4 of the Penneo API collection.

To download as JSON instead of PDF, change the `Accept` header to `application/json`.

exec: throw new Error('accessToken is not set. Run "1. Get Access Token" first (token expires after 10 minutes).');
